name: Grok Build
summary: xAI's first agentic coding CLI — spawns up to 8 concurrent agents that plan, search docs, write files, and run shell commands; powered by Grok 4.3 beta with a 2M-token context; beta is SuperGrok Heavy-only at $300/mo, with a $99/mo intro deal for the first six months

name: ChatGPT Personal Finance + Plaid
summary: ChatGPT Pro can now link bank, brokerage, and card accounts through Plaid across 12,000+ institutions — get a live dashboard of portfolio, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming bills, plus tailored answers grounded in your actual numbers; preview on web and iOS for US Pro users, read-only access

name: Codex in ChatGPT Mobile
summary: Codex lands in the ChatGPT mobile app on iOS and Android — kick off coding tasks, review diffs, redirect runs, and approve agent actions from your phone while Codex executes on a connected Mac; preview across all plans including free, with 4M+ weekly Codex users

name: Raindrop Workshop
summary: open-source local debugger and eval tool for AI agents — streams every token, tool call, and decision to a localhost dashboard the moment it happens, then writes traces to a single SQL file your coding agent can query to write its own evals; MIT-licensed, one-line install, works with Vercel AI SDK, OpenAI Agents, Anthropic SDK, LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, Pydantic AI

name: Cursor 3.4
summary: ships dev environment configuration for cloud agent fleets — multi-repo workspaces, Dockerfile-based envs with build secrets for private registries, and a new PR review experience; click "Build in Parallel" and Cursor identifies independent parts of a plan and runs them simultaneously via async subagents

name: Claude for Small Business
summary: packaged Claude for solopreneurs and small teams — prebuilt agentic workflows and one-click connectors into QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365 to handle payroll, month-end close, invoice chasing, and campaign creation; bundled with a free AI fluency course

name: Realtime API GA + GPT-Realtime-2
summary: voice AI exits beta — GPT-Realtime-2 brings GPT-5-class reasoning to speech with 128K context, parallel tool calls, and narrated 'let me check that' preambles; ships alongside GPT-Realtime-Translate (70 input / 13 output languages at $0.034/min) and GPT-Realtime-Whisper streaming transcription at $0.017/min for live captions and meeting notes

name: HiDream-O1-Image
summary: open-source 8B image model that beats 32B FLUX.2 on five standard benchmarks — works directly in pixel space with no VAE or separate text encoder, supports text-to-image, editing, and subject-driven personalization up to 2048×2048; ships with a reasoning prompt agent that thinks through layout and physics before drawing, MIT-licensed for commercial use, weights on Hugging Face
